findings.title: Handler must not call more than one repository/service method directly
findings.issue: Handler must not call more than one repository/service method directly (deterministic pre-filter; LLM disabled).
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a service / use-case layer (e.g. UserService.CreateWithProfile) that encapsulates the multi-repository orchestration. The handler calls exactly one service method; the service method calls as many repositories as needed, managing transactions internally.

findings.title: Repository function must contain exactly one database call
findings.issue: Repository function must contain exactly one database call (deterministic pre-filter; LLM disabled).
findings.suggested_fix: Split this function into multiple single-responsibility repository functions, one per DB operation. If the operations are logically atomic, wrap them in a single database transaction and issue the calls through the transaction handle inside a dedicated helper.

findings.title: Database call must not appear inside a for/range loop (N+1 anti-pattern)
findings.issue: Database call must not appear inside a for/range loop (N+1 anti-pattern) (deterministic pre-filter; LLM disabled).
findings.suggested_fix: Collect all required identifiers first, then issue a single bulk query using IN ($1, $2, ...) or pgx SendBatch. Alternatively, use a JOIN or a CTE to retrieve all related data in one round-trip.
